---
title: "New Zealand Agriculture Production"
date: 2026-02-17
author: "Cole Baril"
description: "How many sheep are there per person in New Zealand?"
categories: [agriculture, sheep, livestock]
image: preview.png
---
## Load Packages
```{r, load_packages}
require(pacman)
p_load(tidytuesdayR, magick, tidyverse, janitor, here, trashpanda, ggside)
options(scipen = 999)
```
## Data
```{r, tt_data}
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2026-02-17')
dataset <- tuesdata$dataset

nz_pop <- read_csv(here("2026/2026-02-17 - New Zealand Agriculture Production/nz_pop.csv")) |> 
  select(1:2) |> clean_names()
```
## Data Cleaning
```{r, cleaning}
animals <- dataset |> 
  filter(measure %in% c("Total Sheep",
                        "Total Cattle",
                        "Number of alpacas",
                        "Total goats",
                        "Total Pigs",
                        "Total Deer"
                        )) |> 

  left_join(nz_pop, by = c("year_ended_june" = "year")) |> 
  drop_na(population) |> 
  mutate(animal_people = value / population) |> 
  mutate(value_label = str_remove(value_label, "Number of"),
         value_label = str_to_title(value_label))
```
## Plot
```{r, plot, fig.align='center'}
plot <- animals |> 
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = year_ended_june, y = animal_people, colour = value_label),
            linewidth = 1.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = add_commas) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Number of Livestock per Person in New Zealand",
       title = "Fewer Sheep per Person, Still the Top Livestock") +
  scale_colour_viridis_d("Animal", end = 0.9) +
  theme_cole(remove_grid = TRUE) +
  theme(legend.position = "inside",
        legend.position.inside = c(0.75, 0.75)) +
  add_caption_cwb()

  

# Save and display images
current_dir <- dirname(knitr::current_input())
plot_name <- "nz_livestock.png"

ggsave(plot = plot, 
       dpi = "screen",
       width = 14,
       height = 12,
       device = ragg::agg_png,
       filename = file.path(current_dir, plot_name))


# Read the big plot
img <- image_read(file.path(current_dir, plot_name))

# Force 16:9 aspect ratio with minimal padding
# Target size: 1200x675 px (16:9)
img_card <- image_scale(img, "1200x675")           # scale to fit inside 16:9
img_card <- image_extent(
  img_card,
  geometry = "1200x675",
  gravity = "center"
)

# Save as card preview
image_write(img_card, path = file.path(current_dir, "preview.png"))

knitr::include_graphics(
  file.path(current_dir, plot_name)
)

```

> Interestingly, despite the drastic decrease in the proportion of sheep to people, 
> other livestock volumes have not increased.


## References
```{r, ref, echo = TRUE, results = 'asis', comment = NA}
trashpanda::cite_packages(format = "rmd")
```
